waf.ipset.ipsetdescriptors.Value.Description: Specify an IPv4 address by using CIDR notation. For example:  + To configure AWS WAF to allow, block, or count requests that originated from the IP address 192.0.2.44, specify `192.0.2.44/32`.+ To configure AWS WAF to allow, block, or count requests that originated from IP addresses from 192.0.2.0 to 192.0.2.255, specify `192.0.2.0/24`.For more information about CIDR notation, see the Wikipedia entry [Classless Inter-Domain Routing](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Classless_Inter-Domain_Routing).  Specify an IPv6 address by using CIDR notation. For example:  + To configure AWS WAF to allow, block, or count requests that originated from the IP address 1111:0000:0000:0000:0000:0000:0000:0111, specify `1111:0000:0000:0000:0000:0000:0000:0111/128`.+ To configure AWS WAF to allow, block, or count requests that originated from IP addresses 1111:0000:0000:0000:0000:0000:0000:0000 to 1111:0000:0000:0000:ffff:ffff:ffff:ffff, specify `1111:0000:0000:0000:0000:0000:0000:0000/64`.
